pol.n: Valinor — the Realm of the Valar
phases: The Valar remove west after Almaren's ruin; Valinor built behind the Pelóri; the Two Trees made
Darkening of Valinor; Melkor and Ungoliant destroy the Trees
The Host of Valinor marches — the War of Wrath
Valinor removed from the circles of the world at the Downfall

geo: Beyond Belegaer in the uttermost West, walled by the Pelóri, highest of mountains, raised as a fence after Melkor cast down the Lamps; the Calacirya cut to let the Trees' light reach the coast.
Silm 1, 5 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
Valmar of many bells at the plain's heart; the green mound Ezellohar of the Two Trees; Máhanaxar, the Ring of Doom, before its gate.
Silm 1 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
hist: Founded after the ruin of Almaren; lit by the Trees through the Noontide of the Blessed Realm; darkened by Melkor and Ungoliant; closed against the Noldor by the Doom of Mandos; opened once in wrath at Eärendil's embassy; hidden for ever from mortal seas at the Downfall.
Silm 1, 8–9; Akallabêth [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Rule of the Valar in council: Manwë Súlimo is 'first of all Kings, lord of the realm of Arda'; doom is spoken in Máhanaxar; each Vala keeps a demesne (Yavanna's pastures, Oromë's woods, Mandos in the north).
Silm 1; Valaquenta [C]
EVID
rul: Manwë, Elder King, with Varda; the Aratar eight in number.
Valaquenta [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: Festival is canonical institution: the harvest-feast on Taniquetil at which the Trees died, and the appointed praise of Eru; grief too has its craft — Nienna 'teaches pity, and endurance in hope'.
Silm 8; Valaquenta [C?] — quotation not located in the corpus — under review
mil: The realm wars only thrice in arms: the Battle of the Powers, the assault that ended the Siege of Utumno, and the War of Wrath under Eönwë's banner.
Silm 3, 24 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: Embassy is its instrument: Oromë's summons to Cuiviénen; the pardon offered Melkor; Eönwë's judgement over Sauron; Eärendil received as ambassador of Two Kindreds.
Silm 3, 24 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
lan: Valarin among themselves ('the tongues... of the Valar'), Quenya adopted with the Vanyar and Noldor — 'the Valar took to Quenya readily'.
WJ, Quendi & Eldar [C]
EVID
set: Valmar; Tirion in the Calacirya; Alqualondë on the coast; Formenos in the north (Fëanor's exile-fastness); Mandos westward on Ekkaia's shores.
Silm 5–7 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Melkor's parole and treason turn the realm's history; Fëanor's oath is sworn in its darkness; Eärendil's landing breaks its silence.
Silm 7–9, 24 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

aka: city of the Noldor in Eldamar
phases: Built on Túna in the Calacirya by Vanyar and Noldor together; the Vanyar later remove to Taniquetil
The great march of the Noldor out of Tirion behind Fëanor

geo: Set on the green hill Túna in the Calacirya, the only pass of light through the Pelóri; its topmost tower the Mindon Eldaliéva, whose silver lamp shone far into the mists of the sea; the white ways dusted with diamond-dust.
Silm 5 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
hist: Chief city of the Noldor through the Noontide; emptied of nine-tenths of its people in the Flight; under Finarfin it endures, and its host marches at last in the War of Wrath.
Silm 5, 9, 24 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Kingship of the Noldor: Finwë; after his death the crown's contention between Fëanor and Fingolfin splits the people; Finarfin rules the remnant 'and rules the remnant of the Noldor in the deep cleft of the mountains'.
Silm 6–9 [C?] — quotation not located in the corpus — under review
UNRESOLVED
rul: Finwë → (the exile of Fëanor; Fingolfin holds Tirion) → Finarfin, after the Flight.
Silm 6–9 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: The craft-city of the legendarium: gems first made here; 'the Noldor... full of eager thought'; the schism of the princes is a canonical study in faction and pride.
Silm 5–7 [C?] — quotation not located in the corpus — under review
eco: Gem-craft and smith-craft above all — Fëanor's works and the mastery of Mahtan; the diamond-dust of its streets is canonically the Noldor's own strewing.
Silm 5–6 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
lan: Quenya; the Shibboleth of Fëanor (þ→s) is a Tirion political-linguistic quarrel, canon in PM.
PM, Shibboleth [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Fëanor, Fingolfin, Finarfin; Galadriel's departure 'yearning to see the wide unguarded lands'.
Silm 5–9 [C]
EVID

pol.n: Alqualondë — the Haven of the Swans
phases: The Teleri, long sundered on Tol Eressëa, come at last to the coast of Aman and build the Haven
The first Kinslaying: the Noldor seize the white ships

geo: On the coast of Eldamar north of the Calacirya, 'lit with many lamps'; its gate an arch of living rock sea-carved; the strand strewn with gems given by the Noldor.
Silm 5 [C]
EVID
gov: Kingship of Olwë, brother of Elwë-Thingol; his people would not give nor sell their ships against the will of the Valar — the realm's one recorded act of policy, and its costliest.
Silm 5, 9 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
rul: Olwë, from the founding onward; no successor is ever named.
Silm 5 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: Mariners and singers — 'the Sea-elves... they made music by the shore'; the swanships their chief works and chief love.
eco: Ship-craft and pearl-gathering: 'pearls they won from the sea'; their havens' wealth was of the waters.
Silm 5 [C]
EVID
mil: Unarmed by custom: at the Kinslaying they fought with 'slender bows' and ships' gear against mailed Noldor — the corpus records no army before or after.
Silm 9 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: Kinship diplomacy: Olwë refuses Fëanor citing fealty to the Valar; the Teleri's grief closes their havens to the Exiles for an Age.
Silm 9 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Olwë; Eärwen his daughter, mother of Galadriel — through whom the Kinslaying runs down all Galadriel's story.
Silm 5; PM [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

phases: Elwë and Melian gather the Eldar of Beleriand; Menegroth delved with Dwarven aid
After the First Battle the Girdle of Melian is raised
Thingol slain in Menegroth; the Girdle withdrawn; the Dwarves sack Menegroth; the Second Kinslaying ends Dior's brief restoration

geo: The forests of Neldoreth and Region about the river Esgalduin, with Nivrim west of Sirion and Aelin-uial of the Twilight Meres; fenced by the Girdle of Melian, 'an unseen wall of shadow and bewilderment'.
EVID
Menegroth, the Thousand Caves, on Esgalduin's south bank — 'the fairest dwelling of any king that has ever been east of the Sea'.
Silm 10 [C]
EVID
hist: Eldest of the realms of Beleriand: from the Sleep of Yavanna through the whole First Age it stands unconquered by Morgoth; it falls not to Angband but to Dwarven vengeance and the Oath of Fëanor.
Silm 10–22 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Absolute kingship of Elu Thingol with Melian as counsellor-queen ('the counsel of Melian' the realm's recurring hinge); marchwardens under captains such as Beleg on the borders; Mablung chief captain within.
Silm 10, 19; CoH [C]
EVID
Recorded law: the ban on Quenya throughout Beleriand after the Kinslaying is a Doriathrin decree obeyed by all the Sindar — a rare example of legislated language-policy in the legendarium.
Silm 15 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
rul: Elu Thingol (with Melian) through the Age; Dior Eluchíl briefly after him.
Silm 4–22 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: High Sindarin culture: Daeron its loremaster devises the Cirth; the realm hosts the greatest tales — Lúthien's dances in Neldoreth, the fostering of Túrin; guest-right and its breach (Túrin and Saeros) are told in juridical detail, including a formal pardon.
Silm 10, 19; CoH 5 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
eco: Dwarven trade is foundational: Menegroth delved for payment in pearls ('the pearl Nimphelos'); weapons and mail bought from Nogrod and Belegost; the remaking of Nauglamír the fatal last commission.
Silm 10, 22 [C?] — quotation not located in the corpus — under review
UNRESOLVED
mil: Marchwarden system behind an enchanted frontier; bows and the sword-lore of Beleg; Doriath fights the First Battle before the Girdle, sends no host to the Nirnaeth (the ban of Thingol), and falls only when the fence is down.
Silm 10, 20, 22 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: Estrangement as policy: the ban on the sons of Fëanor's tongue; refusal of the Union of Maedhros save Beleg and Mablung 'so long as they served not Fëanor's sons'; the bride-price of the Silmaril the realm's most fateful treaty.
Silm 15, 19–20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
lan: Doriathrin Sindarin, the prestige speech of Beleriand; Cirth invented here by Daeron.
Silm 10; LotR App E [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
set: Menegroth; the guarded bridge of Esgalduin; Hírilorn's great beech; the fords of Aros and the march-forts are named only as 'the north-marches'.
Silm 10, 19 [C?] — quotation not located in the corpus — under review
UNRESOLVED
chr: Thingol & Melian, Lúthien & Beren, Túrin fostered and outlawed, Beleg, Dior — half the Silmarillion's matter passes through the Thousand Caves.
Silm 10–22 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

phases: Turgon, guided by Ulmo, finds Tumladen and builds the city in secret
The host of Gondolin issues to the Nirnaeth — the secrecy half-broken
Betrayed by Maeglin; the city falls at the Gates of Summer

geo: The hidden vale of Tumladen in the Encircling Mountains, entered only by the Dry River and the Seven Gates; the city on the hill Amon Gwareth; Ulmo's power on Sirion its outer shield.
Silm 15–16; UT, Of Tuor [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
hist: Built in secrecy over two generations of the sun; endures longest of the Noldor realms; falls last of the great kingdoms, by treason from within.
Silm 15, 23 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Kingship of Turgon; the Twelve Houses of the Gondothlim (the Kings, the Wing, the Mole, the Swallow, the Heavenly Arch, the Pillar, the Tower of Snow, the Tree, the Golden Flower, the Fountain, the Harp, the Hammer of Wrath) marshal both city and army — the fullest constitutional detail of any Elven city, given in the early Fall of Gondolin.
BoLT2/FoG [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
Law of the realm: none who found the way in might leave — the ordinance that traps Eöl and dooms Maeglin's line.
Silm 16 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
rul: Turgon son of Fingolfin; Maeglin's claim by treason never crowned; Tuor and Idril lead the remnant out.
Silm 16, 23 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: The city kept the memory of Tirion 'and the trees that Turgon wrought of gold and silver'; the festival Tarnin Austa (Gates of Summer) with its silent vigil; Nost-na-Lothion, the Birth of Flowers.
eco: Self-contained mountain economy; smith-craft famous — the making of Glamdring and Orcrist ('made in Gondolin for the goblin-wars') and Maeglin's mining of the Echoriath are its recorded industries.
Hobbit 3; Silm 16 [C]
EVID
mil: The Houses field distinct arms (the Fountain with silver and diamonds, the Hammer with maces); Ecthelion slays Gothmog at the King's fountain; Glorfindel's stand in Cirith Thoronath covers the retreat.
BoLT2/FoG; Silm 23 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: Isolation as doctrine, against Ulmo's own later counsel through Tuor to abandon the city; the refusal is the realm's tragedy: 'love not too well the work of thy hands'.
UT, Of Tuor; Silm 23 [C]
EVID
lan: Sindarin in daily use, Quenya kept 'in the king's house' — the one place the banned High Speech lived on in Beleriand.
Silm 15; PM [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
set: The Seven Gates of the Orfalch Echor; the Place of the Fountain; the Tower of the King; Idril's secret way.
UT, Of Tuor; BoLT2 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Turgon, Idril & Tuor, Maeglin, Ecthelion, Glorfindel; Eärendil born here — the survival of the world's hope threads through its fall.
Silm 16, 23 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

phases: Finrod, shown Menegroth, delves Nargothrond in the caves of Narog
Finrod goes with Beren; Celegorm and Curufin usurp the people's love; Orodreth rules after
The bridge and the open battle of Tumhalad; Glaurung sacks the city

geo: The caves above Narog at the doors of the Ringwil, behind the guarded plain Talath Dirnen; the realm ran from Nenning to Sirion's west bank, greatest in extent of the Noldor lands.
Silm 13–14 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
hist: Founded on Doriath's model; long the strength of the west; policy breaks between secrecy and the open war urged by Túrin — the bridge over Narog is the realm's self-inflicted wound.
Silm 13, 21; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Kingship with a council that can overrule the king in fact: Celegorm and Curufin sway 'the most part of the people' against Finrod, who casts down his crown; under Orodreth, Túrin governs war-policy without title — 'though he would not take the crown'.
Silm 19, 21 [C]
EVID
rul: Finrod Felagund → Orodreth (under the shadow of the Fëanorians' expulsion) — with Túrin as war-leader at the end.
Silm 19, 21 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: Hewer of caves (Felagund = 'Hewer of Caves', given by the Dwarves); the Ring of Barahir's oath-culture begins here; secrecy shaped all custom — hunters that left no trail, 'no man of the realm' seen abroad.
eco: Delved wealth and Dwarf-wrought treasure: the Nauglamír made for Finrod, 'most renowned of the works of the Dwarves in the Elder Days'; treasure enough that Mîm's curse and Glaurung's hoard-lust both fasten on it.
Silm 13, 22; CoH [C]
EVID
mil: Doctrine of ambush and the guarded plain — watchtower Amon Ethir raised; the abandonment of secrecy for pitched battle at Tumhalad is the corpus's clearest canonical strategy-debate, argued by Gwindor against Túrin.
Silm 21; CoH 11 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: Bound by Barahir's ring and Finrod's oath to the house of Bëor; wrecked by the Fëanorian brothers' ambition; refuses the Union of Maedhros save a small company under Gwindor.
Silm 18–20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
lan: Sindarin (the ban observed); Finrod alone of kings learns the tongues of Men, and first interprets them.
Silm 17 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
set: The city in the caves; the great bridge (fatal); Amon Ethir the Spyhill; the towers of the Nenning watched the havens once.
Silm 21; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Finrod & Beren, Celegorm & Curufin, Orodreth, Gwindor, Túrin & Glaurung, Finduilas — the realm is the stage of both great oaths and their wreck.
Silm 18–21; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

phases: The Noldor land in Mithrim; Fingolfin holds the north-west
The Bragollach beaten off at Eithel Sirion; Fingolfin falls before Angband's gate
After the Nirnaeth, Morgoth's Easterlings are penned in Hithlum over the Edain's remnant

geo: Between Ered Wethrin and Ered Lómin, cool and rain-swept, holding Lake Mithrim; Dor-lómin its southern fief; the fortress Barad Eithel at Sirion's well its eastern door.
Silm 13–14 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
hist: Seat of the High Kingship of the Noldor in exile through the Long Peace and both great defeats; its fall at the Nirnaeth ends organized Elvish power in the north-west.
Silm 13–20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: High Kingship: Fingolfin, then Fingon; Dor-lómin granted as lordship to the House of Hador — the clearest canonical vassalage among Elves and Men ('Dor-lómin was given to Hador').
Silm 17–18 [C?] — quotation not located in the corpus — under review
UNRESOLVED
rul: Fingolfin (F.A. 1–456) → Fingon (456–473); Hador's line as lords of Dor-lómin under them.
Silm 18, 20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
mil: The siege-line of Ered Wethrin manned from Barad Eithel; cavalry on Ard-galen until the Flame; Fingon's host is the western pincer of the Nirnaeth's grand design.
Silm 18, 20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: The Union with Maedhros's eastern host; the alliance of the Edain sealed by land-grant and mutual oath; broken at last by the treason of Ulfang's Men — 'the treachery of Men' entering the histories.
Silm 20 [C]
EVID
lan: Sindarin and Quenya among the lords; the Edain of Dor-lómin adopt Sindarin 'and forsook their own tongue' in part.
Silm 17; PM [C]
EVID
set: Barad Eithel; the halls of Mithrim; Húrin's house in Dor-lómin under Amon Darthir.
Silm 18; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Fingolfin's duel; Fingon and the rescue of Maedhros; Húrin and Morwen of Dor-lómin — the captivity of Húrin binds this land's fate to the Narn.
Silm 13, 18, 20; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

phases: Dor-lómin given to Hador Lórindol by Fingolfin
The Easterlings possess the land; the Edain enslaved
Túrin's generation scattered; Morwen and Niënor flee

geo: Southern Hithlum under Ered Wethrin; Amon Darthir above Húrin's fields; cold, misted, horse-rearing country.
Silm 14; CoH 1 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Hereditary lordship under the High King: Hador → Galdor → Húrin (with Huor); after the Nirnaeth, Brodda and the incomer Easterlings rule by seizure — occupation government shown in detail in the Narn (thralls, seized women, the hall-order of Brodda).
Silm 18, 20; CoH 12 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
rul: Hador, Galdor, Húrin — 'mightiest of the warriors of mortal Men'; Brodda the usurper by force.
Silm 18, 20; CoH [C]
EVID
cul: The golden-haired Third House; the tongue of Hador's folk becomes the root of Adûnaic and thence of Westron — this small fief is the linguistic ancestor of half the later world.
Silm 17; PM; SD [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
mil: The rearguard of the Nirnaeth: Húrin's stand at Rivil's marsh — 'Aurë entuluva!' seventy times — the most-cited last stand in the corpus.
Silm 20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
lan: Taliska-descended Mannish speech alongside adopted Sindarin; the women kept the old tongue, per the Narn's detail of Túrin's household.
CoH 1; PM [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Húrin, Morwen, Túrin, Niënor — the Narn's whole engine; Rían and Huor's line running to Tuor and so to Eärendil.
CoH; Silm 20–23 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

pol.panel: The Edain of the Three Houses

aka: Dorthonion's fief of the First House
phases: Ladros in north-east Dorthonion granted to the sons of Bëor by the sons of Finarfin
The Bragollach burns Dorthonion; Barahir's outlawry ends at Tarn Aeluin

geo: The north-eastern slopes of Dorthonion; earlier Estolad ('the Encampment') in East Beleriand was the First House's landless first home.
Silm 17 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Lordship under Finrod's house: Boromir, Bregor, Bregolas and Barahir named lords; after the burning, Barahir's twelve companions are an outlaw-fellowship whose names the corpus preserves entire.
Silm 17–19 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: The First House 'dark or brown of hair, eager of mind'; from it come Beren, and through Beren all the Half-elven lines; the ring of Barahir, Finrod's oath-token, becomes the longest-lived heirloom of Men.
mil: Bregolas falls in the Bragollach; Barahir saves Finrod at the Fen of Serech — the oath that follows is the fief's chief 'treaty'; the last twelve wage guerrilla war from Tarn Aeluin until betrayal.
Silm 18–19 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Barahir, Beren, Emeldir the Man-hearted leading the women and children out — an explicit canonical case of female war-leadership.
Silm 18–19 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

pol.panel: The Edain of the Three Houses

phases: The Haladin under Haleth remove to Brethil, by leave of Thingol
Brethil holds the Crossings of Teiglin; Túrin's last deeds at Cabed-en-Aras
The Wanderings of Húrin wreck the chieftain's house (HoME layer)
WotJ, Wanderings of Húrin [C — unpublished layer]

geo: The wood between Teiglin and Sirion, its heart Amon Obel and Ephel Brandir; the Crossings of Teiglin its strategic door.
Silm 21; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Not a kingship: the Haladin follow chosen chieftains (Haleth a woman first of them), and WotJ shows a full folk-moot — the Doom-ring of Brethil trying Húrin — the most detailed 'parliamentary' scene in the legendarium.
Silm 17; WotJ [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
Halad succession: Haldir, Handir, Brandir the lame; the people can and do gainsay their chieftain (Brandir overruled on war).
Silm 20–21; CoH [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
cul: A folk 'apart, and were known as the People of Haleth'; with them the Drúedain dwell — the only recorded Edain–Drúadan cohabitation; woodland burial and the mound of Túrin their monuments.
mil: Forest war: the ambush at the Crossings; Haleth's defence of the stockade; Handir falls to Orc-raids; Túrin leads and breaks the Glaurung-terror at Cabed-en-Aras.
Silm 17, 20–21; CoH 17 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
dip: Haleth's folk hold Brethil free, on the one condition that they guard the Crossings of Teiglin — “that she should dwell free in Brethil, upon the condition only that her people should guard the Crossings of Teiglin”. The grant was obtained for her by Felagund: Thingol “would have denied it to Haleth”.
Silm 17 [C]
EVID
chr: Haleth; Brandir; Túrin as Turambar and Níniel — the Narn's terrible end plays out here; Húrin's coming breaks the polity itself.
CoH; WotJ [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE

pol.panel: The Edain of the Three Houses

phases: Círdan's people remain by the shores for love of Ossë; the walled Havens built
The Havens besieged and razed the year after the Nirnaeth; remnant to Balar and Sirion

geo: The western shore-lands about the mouths of Brithon and Nenning; the two walled havens; Barad Nimras raised by Finrod against sea-assault that never came.
Silm 14 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: Lordship of Círdan the Shipwright, 'lord of the Falas'; after the fall he leads the remnant — the one Beleriand polity whose ruler and people survive into every later Age.
Silm 10, 20 [C]
EVID
eco: The corpus's chief shipwrights: ships for Turgon's search westward, the rescue-fleet at the Nirnaeth's eve, later the vessels of the Isle of Balar.
Silm 20; UT [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
mil: Walled-port defence; ship-borne relief of Eglarest's siege in the First Battle era; fell only to the full land-strength of Angband after the Nirnaeth.
Silm 10, 20 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
chr: Círdan, whose foresight ('the ships... a way will open') threads the whole legendarium to the Grey Havens.
Silm 20; LotR [C]
EVID

phases: After Thingol's word, the sons of Fëanor remove east; Maedhros holds Himring
The Bragollach: Himring alone stands; the Gap and Lothlann lost
After the Nirnaeth the March is broken; the brothers scatter to Ossiriand's eaves

geo: The Hill of Himring 'ever-cold' commanding the Pass of Aglon and Maglor's Gap; Lothlann's plain northward; the outworks stretch from Aglon to the feet of Ered Luin.
Silm 14 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
gov: A war-lordship of six princes under Maedhros's primacy: Celegorm and Curufin in Aglon (till 455), Maglor in the Gap, Caranthir by Helevorn, Amrod and Amras in the south-east hunting-lands.
Silm 14 [I-H] — the archive's synthesis of canon, not a quotation
PROSE
mil: The cavalry screen of Lothlann; Himring's fortress-doctrine proven in the Bragollach; the Union of Maedhros is the March's grand strategy — and its ruin at the Nirnaeth by the treason it recruited.
